flags.concern: Video-editor shell: the plan’s width/aspect-based condensed threshold ignores the existing standard-layout maximize-timeline path, which already fixes the preview row at 180px. In this repo that means portrait timelines could be forced into condensed mode on normal desktop heights, effectively taking away the non-condensed maximize-timeline workflow unless the plan explicitly scopes the new threshold around `isTimelineMaximized` or otherwise accepts that behavior change.
flags.evidence: The current condensed switch is a simple height check at `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:133-146`, while the standard layout still allows `isTimelineMaximized ? `${MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT}px auto 1fr`` at `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:150-152`. The standard grid keeps the wide preview column in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:428-436`, and the preview component itself renders inside a fixed-height container (`min-h-[220px]`) at `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx:92-95`.
